bottlenecks.name: 2.5D advanced packaging capacity
bottlenecks.technical_reason: AI accelerators require advanced integration that depends on constrained packaging capacity, substrates, interposers, bonding, inspection, and yield learning.

companies.name: ASE
risks: less direct exposure to leading-edge AI packages

core_question: Does advanced packaging remain a structural AI bottleneck after the initial GPU shortage phase?
counter_theses: Capacity additions could shift the bottleneck away from CoWoS to downstream deployment.
Alternative package architectures could reduce dependence on one packaging flow.
Customer bargaining power could limit margin expansion for enablers.
drivers: Larger accelerator packages increase HBM attach and interposer area.
Chiplet architectures require dense package-level interconnect.
Training and inference clusters require higher performance per package and per watt.
HBM growth couples memory bottlenecks to package capacity.

claims: AI demand has created pressure on advanced process nodes and 2.5D/3D packaging.
evidence.title: TrendForce discusses AI-driven advanced packaging constraints

claims: AI accelerator and networking demand implies continued pressure on package-level integration capacity.
evidence.title: NVIDIA first quarter fiscal 2027 results

mechanism: AI accelerators require dense package-level integration of compute, memory, and chiplets. As package size, HBM stack count, and interconnect density rise, packaging capacity and yield learning become deployment constraints.

profit_pools.name: leading-edge package integration
profit_pools.rationale: Packaging slots that can support AI accelerator and HBM integration are scarce and customer-qualified.

profit_pools.name: equipment and inspection for yield expansion
profit_pools.rationale: Capacity growth requires tools and inspection processes that improve yield and throughput.

scenarios.description: Accelerator package complexity rises faster than packaging capacity.
scenarios.name: bull
triggers: accelerator package area and HBM stack counts rise

scenarios.description: Capacity additions and alternative packaging paths reduce scarcity.
scenarios.name: bear

segments.name: Foundry advanced packaging
segments.role: Provide CoWoS-like integration for accelerators, HBM, and chiplets.

segments.name: OSAT and assembly
segments.role: Support assembly, test, and packaging expansion outside leading foundries.

segments.name: Substrates and interposers
segments.role: Provide physical package foundations and signal routing.

segments.name: Packaging equipment and inspection
segments.role: Enable capacity expansion and yield control for advanced packages.

thesis: Advanced packaging remains a structural AI bottleneck because high-end accelerators increasingly depend on close integration of compute die, HBM stacks, interposers, substrates, and high-speed package interconnect. The bottleneck can migrate from foundry packaging slots to tools, substrates, inspection, and thermal/mechanical integration.
title: CoWoS and Advanced Packaging Bottleneck
tracking_signals: Foundry advanced packaging capex and capacity targets.
ABF substrate and interposer supply conditions.
Accelerator package roadmap and HBM stack count.
